what: Writing a Rust kernel — the hard rules
title: AGENTS.md — operating manual for coding agents in the Razer repo
text: Read src/rust/src/steps.rs first; mirror . Full detail + LLM prompt template extending article (§5–§7). contract: #[extendr] fn; args = agent Columns + count: i32 + n_nodes: i32 + &Distributions. Take Columns & (read) / &mut (write); slice [..count]. Storage types: state u8, timer u16, nodeid u16. Use matching accessor — as_u8_mut / as_u16_mut / as_u16 (also as_u32 / as_i32_mut / as_f64). panic dtype mismatch. Return per-node counts — Vec<i32> named List integer[n_nodes]. Never write node census inside kernel; R caller applies counts move_count. Parallelize par_chunks_mut(rng::RNG_CHUNK) + private per-node Vec<i64> reduced summing (shared writes). RNG: let base = rng::next_call_base(); parallel fan-, rng::chunk_rng(base, chunk_index) inside chunk. keeps seeded run reproducible independent thread count — never use thread_rng() seed per-thread. Draw uniform rng.gen::<f64>() (use rand::Rng;). Guard u16 timer decrements: t[j] > 0 { t[j] -= 1; } testing == 0. Validate inputs assert!. Register function file’s extendr_module! { … }, ensure module listed src/rust/src/lib.rs (mod x; use x;). New file ⇒ add . Ship test (see Testing) run devtools::document() devtools::test(). even need Rust? change requires novel per-agent computation every tick composition existing kernels (step_timer_expire(_set), move_count, births/mortality/import_infections, bincount family, squash). Vaccination, waning, quarantine, vital dynamics, imports, age-targeted reports, time/space-varying drivers pure-R callbacks — Rust. want write per-agent loop R, round-trip whole agent Column R every tick, signal write kernel instead. See extending article §3.

what: Modeling convention: the Column kernels and per-tick ordering
title: razer — project notes for Claude
text: Models composed Column-based per-tick kernels Column buffers (allocate_scalar / allocate_vector). Agent state u8; per-agent timer u16 everywhere (maternal / immunity periods exceed u8’s 255). Kernels mutate per-agent arrays RETURN per-node counts; model applies deltas census maintains. kernel takes census/flow buffers, model allocates states . flow : carry_forward(_states) copies census column t → t+1, model applies kernel’s returned counts column t+1 move_count(, , counts, t) (/may NULL one-sided moves — death decrements , birth increments ). calc_foi(, N, beta, seasonality, network, foi, t) — per-node force infection (writes foi report Column; one kernel still reads/writes Columns). transmission(state, timer, nodeid, count, foi, t, to_state, duration) → counts — S→to_state (E ), sets u16 timer; returns new infections per node. transmission_si(state, nodeid, count, foi, t) → counts — S→absorbing (timer); SI model. step_si(…, inf_dur) → list(waned, onset) — M→S, E→. step_sir(…, inf_dur, absorbing_state) → list(waned, onset, cleared) — adds →absorbing_state (S R). step_sirs(…, inf_dur, imm_dur) → list(waned, onset, recovered, waned_r) — adds →R (sets immunity timer) R→S. births(…) → list(count, born), mortality(…) → list(m, s, e, , r), constant_pop_vitals_sir(…) (constant-pop convenience; still writes census), import_infections(…). eight-model menagerie — every model transmission + step kernel: step kernel single pass branching agent’s entry state (leads M→S, model can add maternal state), just-entered timed state never decremented tick — residency artifact (duration − 1) avoided structurally. One ordering every model — always realize full beta · D, never beta · (D − 1). tick runs, every model: carry_forward(_states) → step_* (apply counts) → calc_foi → transmission (apply counts) calc_foi placed immediately transmission step kernel . works calc_foi reads settled start--interval infectious census [t] (working column t+1 tick’s step transmission build), value depend step kernel runs. agent enters one census column infected (via either step kernel’s E→transmission’s S→) recovers D columns later, contributes FOI exactly D columns occupies — R0 = beta · D direct S→E-entry, special-casing residency artifact. (Historical note: calc_foi used read [t+1], forced different ordering per family; reading [t] removed footgun.) Validated examples/sir_attack_fraction.R / seir_attack_fraction.R (match Kermack–McKendrick final size R0 = beta · D). See examples/engwal_measles.R full M-S-E--R model simple_sir.R / endemic_sir.R spatial Column SIR. Higher-level helpers. run_model(scenario, model, …) wires whole menagerie correct order returns model environment bundling $people, $nodes, $network, $carry; seeds states model (E//R column scenario supplies), records per-node flows incidence / onset / recovery / waning applicable, takes optional init(model) plus three per-tick callbacks step_enter (start) / step_update (step kernel calc_foi) / step_exit (end tick) user extension. squash(people) compacts dead agents ; calc_capacity(...) bounds cumulative-births capacity (reclaim) calc_capacity_cdr(...) bounds peak-living capacity squash-reclaimed long runs. binning family bincount() / bincount_wt() (weighted) / bincount_where() (predicate- filtered, count-aware) / bincount_where_wt() (weighted + filtered).

title: Epidemic final size: R₀, the attack fraction, and Kermack–McKendrick
text: textbook SIR model splits population Susceptible, Infectious, Recovered, mass-action transmission exponential recovery: dSdt=−βSIN,dIdt=βSIN−γI,dRdt=γI,\frac{dS}{dt} = -\beta \frac{S }{N}, \qquad \frac{dI}{dt} = \beta \frac{S }{N} - \gamma , \qquad \frac{dR}{dt} = \gamma , β\beta transmission rate, 1/γ=D1/\gamma = D mean infectious period, basic reproduction number R0=βDR_0 = \beta D — expected number secondary cases one infectious individual fully susceptible population. classic result (Kermack & McKendrick, 1927): single epidemic closed, well-mixed, fully susceptible population infects deterministic fraction population — attack fraction AA — given transcendental relation =1−e−R0A\boxed{\,= 1 - e^{-R_0 }\,} (R0≤1R_0 \le 1 root =0A=0: epidemic.) derivation divides dS/dRdS/dR get S=S0e−R0R/NS = S_0 e^{-R_0 R/N}, sets t→∞t\\infty ∞=0I_\infty = 0. Notice AA depends R0R_0only — β\beta DD separately, latent period. ’ll test Razer’s stochastic, agent-based simulation.

what: How Razer builds an SIR — and why R₀ = β·D exactly
title: Epidemic final size: R₀, the attack fraction, and Kermack–McKendrick
text: run_model() advances agent-based SIR: every individual row Rust-owned Column arrays (u8 state, u16 recovery timer, u16 nodeid). tick runs, one fixed order, subtle part discrete time. calc_foi computes per-node force infection settled start--interval infectious census [t][t] (half-updated working column), agent contributes transmission exactly DD ticks infectious. makes realized reproduction number full R0=βDR_0 = \beta D — β(D−1)\beta(D-1), --one naive “decrement--count” loop produce. run_model derives β=R0/D¯\beta = R_0 / \overline{D} us, pass R0R_0 directly.
code: carry_forward → step (recover I→R) → calc_foi → transmission (infect S→I) N <- 1e5L; D <- 10L m <- run_model(data.frame(population = N, I = 100L), "SIR", nticks = 160L,                r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = D, seed = 1L)   # beta = 2.5 / 10 = 0.25 traj <- cbind(S = m$nodes$S$values()[, 1], I = m$nodes$I$values()[, 1], R = m$nodes$R$values()[, 1]) matplot(0:159, traj, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = c("#2c7fb8", "#d7301f", "#238b45"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "agents", main = sprintf("SIR, R0 = beta*D = %.1f", 2.5)) legend("right", c("S", "I", "R"), col = c("#2c7fb8", "#d7301f", "#238b45"), lwd = 2, bty = "n") cat(sprintf("attack fraction here = 1 - S_inf/N = %.3f  (K-M predicts ~0.89)\n",             1 - tail(traj[, "S"], 1) / N)) ## attack fraction here = 1 - S_inf/N = 0.892  (K-M predicts ~0.89)

title: Epidemic final size: R₀, the attack fraction, and Kermack–McKendrick
text: sweep R0R_0, run completion, read =1−S∞/NA = 1 - S_\infty/N. (Agent-based run_model early stop, just use horizon long enough epidemic finish.) overlay Kermack–McKendrick curve.  agreement thousandths across supercritical range — strong validation agent model realizes R0=βDR_0 = \beta D. Just threshold (R0≈1R_0 \approx 1) finite seeded population gives small positive attack fraction deterministic theory predicts zero: expected near-critical, finite-size stochastic behavior.
code: km_attack <- function(R0) if (R0 <= 1) 0 else   uniroot(function(A) 1 - exp(-R0 * A) - A, c(1e-9, 1 - 1e-12))$root  sim_attack <- function(R0, n = 1e5L, D = 10L, horizon = 1500L) {   mm <- run_model(data.frame(population = n, I = 50L), "SIR", nticks = horizon,                   r0 = R0, infectious_period = D, seed = 1L)   S <- mm$nodes$S$values()[, 1]; 1 - S[length(S)] / n }  R0_grid <- seq(0.5, 4, by = 0.25) sim <- vapply(R0_grid, sim_attack, numeric(1)) km  <- vapply(R0_grid, km_attack,  numeric(1))  plot(R0_grid, km, type = "l", lwd = 2.5, ylim = c(0, 1),      xlab = expression(R[0]), ylab = "attack fraction",      main = "Razer SIR (points) vs Kermack–McKendrick (line)") points(R0_grid, sim, pch = 19, col = "#d7301f"); abline(v = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey") cat(sprintf("max |sim - theory| for R0 >= 1.5: %.4f\n", max(abs(sim - km)[R0_grid >= 1.5]))) ## max |sim - theory| for R0 >= 1.5: 0.0035

what: SEIR: a latent stage delays but does not change the final size
title: Epidemic final size: R₀, the attack fraction, and Kermack–McKendrick
text: Adding Exposed (latent) state — run_model(model = "SEIR", incubation_period = …) — inserts non-infectious delay S→E→→RS \E \\R. slows epidemic’s timing , latent period doesn’t change many people one case infects, leaves final size obeying relation R0=βDR_0 = \beta D (infectious period alone).
code: sim_attack_seir <- function(R0, n = 1e5L, D = 10L, De = 5L, horizon = 2000L) {   mm <- run_model(data.frame(population = n, I = 50L), "SEIR", nticks = horizon, r0 = R0,                   infectious_period = D, incubation_period = De, seed = 1L)   S <- mm$nodes$S$values()[, 1]; 1 - S[length(S)] / n } sim_se <- vapply(R0_grid, sim_attack_seir, numeric(1)) plot(R0_grid, km, type = "l", lwd = 2.5, ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = expression(R[0]),      ylab = "attack fraction", main = "SEIR matches the same final-size curve") points(R0_grid, sim_se, pch = 17, col = "#7570b3"); abline(v = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey") legend("bottomright", c("Kermack–McKendrick", "Razer SEIR"), pch = c(NA, 17),        lwd = c(2.5, NA), col = c("black", "#7570b3"), bty = "n")

Razer provides , ported laser.core. disease ; population-building step vital-dynamics measles models build .

what: Ages from a population pyramid (the alias method)
title: Realistic populations: age pyramids and dates of death
text: population pyramid count people age band (often sex). sample million agents’ ages proportion , Razer builds AliasedDistribution — Walker’s alias method, draws arbitrary discrete distribution O(1) per sample O(k) setup, far faster cumulative-sum search. sample_pyramid_ages() wraps : pick band proportion population, uniform day within band’s year range, returning ages days.
code: pyramid    <- load_pyramid_csv(file.path("..", "..", "examples", "data", "pyramid_example.csv"))  # "Age,M,F" bands n_agents   <- 1000000L ages_days  <- sample_pyramid_ages(pyramid, n_agents) ages_years <- ages_days / 365 cat(sprintf("%s agents; ages %.1f–%.1f yr (mean %.1f)\n",             format(n_agents, big.mark = ","), min(ages_years), max(ages_years), mean(ages_years))) ## 1,000,000 agents; ages 0.0–101.0 yr (mean 32.8)

what: A date of death (Kaplan–Meier, conditioned on current age)
title: Realistic populations: age pyramids and dates of death
text: want agent’s age death. Razer’s KaplanMeierEstimator takes life table — cumulative deaths year synthetic cohort — samples age death agent conditioned age alive now, draw never past (essence Kaplan–Meier conditional survival). synthesize life table Gompertz–Makeham per-year mortality hazard q()q() — small baseline, exponential rise age, elevated infant rate — running cohort .
code: n_years <- 106L; age <- 0:(n_years - 1L) q <- pmin(0.0004 + 1e-5 * exp(0.115 * age), 1); q[1] <- 0.02      # hazard; q[1] = infant mortality deaths <- numeric(n_years); alive <- 1e5 for (a in seq_len(n_years)) {                                    # run a cohort through the hazard   d <- if (a == n_years) alive else round(alive * q[a])          # force the last year to clear   deaths[a] <- d; alive <- alive - d } km <- kaplan_meier_estimator(cumsum(deaths))                     # non-decreasing cumulative deaths  death_age_days <- km$predict_age_at_death(ages_days, -1L)        # -1L: use the table's last year death_age_years <- death_age_days / 365 stopifnot(all(death_age_days >= ages_days))                      # nobody dies in the past e0 <- mean(km$predict_age_at_death(integer(1e5L), -1L)) / 365    # life expectancy at birth cat(sprintf("predicted age at death: mean %.1f yr; implied e0 = %.1f yr; mean remaining life %.1f yr\n",             mean(death_age_years), e0, mean(death_age_years - ages_years))) ## predicted age at death: mean 77.0 yr; implied e0 = 73.9 yr; mean remaining life 44.2 yr par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)); br <- seq(0, n_years, 1) hist(ages_years, breaks = br, col = "steelblue", border = "white",      xlab = "age (years)", ylab = "agents", main = "Sampled age structure (the pyramid, realized)") hist(death_age_years, breaks = br, col = "firebrick", border = "white",      xlab = "age at death (years)", ylab = "agents",      main = "Predicted age at death (note the infant spike and the old-age bulk)") abline(v = e0, lty = 2, col = "grey30"); legend("topleft", sprintf("e0 = %.1f yr", e0), lty = 2, bty = "n")

Age-based targeting: bincount_where(nodeid, n_nodes, dob, "gt", tick - 5*365, count) counts (, _wt, weights) -fives per node campaign report.

what: From a single epidemic to a persistent one
title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: final-size notebook showed closed SIR burning susceptibles stopping. Real endemic diseases persist susceptible pool continually replenished — births, loss immunity, migration. Add demographic turnover (birth/death rate μ\mu) SIR system non-trivial endemic equilibrium: S*N=1R0,*N=μβ(R0−1).\frac{S^*}{N} = \frac{1}{R_0}, \qquad \frac{^*}{N} = \frac{\mu}{\beta}\,(R_0 - 1). susceptible fraction settles 1/R01/R_0 — herd-immunity threshold — exactly level effective reproduction number Reff=R0S/NR_\text{eff} = R_0\,S/N equals 1: case replaces , . Push SS 1/R01/R_0 epidemic grows; , shrinks. approach S*S^* series damped epidemic waves.

title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: run_model() closed-population (allocates exactly initial agents). Vital dynamics importation layered callbacks capacity argument: capacity reserves agent slots initial count, import_infections() somewhere put newly-introduced cases (closed run grow). step_exit callback runs tick transmission: constant_pop_vitals_sir applies births = deaths (death reborn susceptible, NN constant), import_infections seeds cases schedule. importation? finite stochastic population can last infectious agent recover chance — fade-— critical community size. trickle imports keeps chain alive observe equilibrium rather extinction. First, factor constant-population vitals + importation reusable pair callbacks (use runs ). watch system approach equilibrium rather land answer, seed population S*S^* — half susceptible, well 1/R0≈0.331/R_0 \approx 0.33 — let relax.  Starting S/N=0.5S/N = 0.5, first epidemic overshoots — driving susceptibles well 1/R01/R_0 — births refill pool, smaller epidemic fires, system spirals S*=N/R0S^* = N/R_0 (dotted line) damped waves, persisting low endemic level instead going extinct. (Seed 1/R01/R_0 line flat tick 0 — system already rest, endemic_sir.R study equilibrium seasonal response directly.)
code: n_nodes <- 2L; pop <- 5e5L; R0 <- 3; cdr <- 20                  # crude death rate (per 1,000/yr) network <- matrix(c(0, 0.01, 0.01, 0), n_nodes, byrow = TRUE)   # 1% cross-coupling  # init + step_exit for an endemic SIR: constant-population births = deaths (each death reborn # susceptible, so N is constant) plus a fixed importation schedule that prevents fade-out. endemic_callbacks <- function(nticks, sched) {   st <- as.integer(sched$tick); sn <- as.integer(sched$node); sc <- as.integer(sched$count)   list(     init = function(model) {       model$nodes$death_rate   <- values_map(cdr / 1000 / 365, nticks, model$nodes$count)       model$nodes$births       <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, model$nodes$count)       model$nodes$deaths       <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, model$nodes$count)       model$nodes$importations <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, model$nodes$count)     },     step_exit = function(model) {       constant_pop_vitals_sir(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,         model$people$count, model$nodes$death_rate, model$nodes$S, model$nodes$I,         model$nodes$R, model$nodes$births, model$nodes$deaths, model$tick)       model$people$count <- import_infections(model$people$state, model$people$timer,         model$people$nodeid, model$people$count, model$nodes$I, model$nodes$importations,         st, sn, sc, dist_gamma(2, 4), model$tick)     }) } nticks   <- 40L * 365L                                              # long enough to settle sched    <- expand.grid(tick = seq(0L, nticks - 2L, 30L), node = 0:1); sched$count <- 5L # Seed ABOVE the equilibrium: half the population susceptible (R = N/2 immune), so S0 = 0.5 > 1/R0. scenario <- data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(100L, n_nodes),                        R = rep(round(0.5 * pop), n_nodes)) cb <- endemic_callbacks(nticks, sched) m <- run_model(scenario, "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = R0, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                network = network, capacity = sum(scenario$population) + sum(sched$count),                seed = 1L, init = cb$init, step_exit = cb$step_exit)  yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 S  <- rowSums(m$nodes$S$values()) / (n_nodes * pop)                 # susceptible FRACTION I  <- rowSums(m$nodes$I$values()) par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) plot(yr, S, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#2c7fb8", ylim = c(0, max(S) * 1.05),      xlab = "year", ylab = "susceptible fraction", main = "Susceptibles overshoot, then damp to S* = 1/R0") abline(h = 1 / R0, lty = 3, col = "#2c7fb8")                        # the equilibrium S*/N = 1/R0 plot(yr, I / 1e3, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f",      xlab = "year", ylab = "infectious (thousands)", main = "Damped epidemic waves to an endemic level")

what: Seasonal forcing: a small wobble, a big response
title: Endemic dynamics: vital turnover, importation, and seasonality
text: Make transmission seasonal passing run_model time-varying seasonality multiplier (values_map-broadcastable shape). endemic system sits critically poised Reff≈1R_\text{eff}\approx 1, even gentle annual sinusoid (±10%\pm 10\% β\beta) drives pronounced, phase-locked annual epidemic waves — resonance phenomenon central dynamics childhood infections like measles. seed equilibrium forced waves stand without startup transient.
code: nticks   <- 10L * 365L sched    <- expand.grid(tick = seq(0L, nticks - 2L, 30L), node = 0:1); sched$count <- 10L scenario <- data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(100L, n_nodes),                        R = rep(pop - round(pop / R0), n_nodes))      # seed S = N/R0 (at equilibrium) amp  <- 0.10 seas <- 1 + amp * sin(2 * pi * (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365)        # length-nticks per-day multiplier cb <- endemic_callbacks(nticks, sched) ms <- run_model(scenario, "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = R0, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                 network = network, seasonality = seas,                 capacity = sum(scenario$population) + sum(sched$count),                 seed = 1L, init = cb$init, step_exit = cb$step_exit) yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) plot(yr, seas, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#e6a000", xlab = "year", ylab = "beta multiplier",      main = sprintf("Seasonal forcing (+/-%.0f%%)", 100 * amp)); abline(h = 1, lty = 3, col = "grey") plot(yr, rowSums(ms$nodes$I$values()) / 1e3, type = "l", lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f",      xlab = "year", ylab = "infectious (thousands)", main = "Phase-locked annual epidemic waves") abline(v = 0:10, col = "grey92")

code: # A vaccinated state, driven entirely from R — no Rust needed. m <- run_model(scenario, "SIR", nticks = 365L, r0 = 2,                infectious_period = 8, extra_states = "V", seed = 1L,                step_exit = function(model) {                  if (model$tick == 30L) {                       # one-time campaign on day 30                    # move some S -> V at 70% coverage in node 0 (illustrative)                    # (use a kernel/report to pick agents; apply the count to the census)                    move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, vaccinated_per_node, model$tick)                  }                })

title: Using and extending Razer: from R callbacks to custom Rust kernels
text: Every Razer agent-loop kernel follows shape. Read src/rust/src/steps.rs (cleanest exemplar) src/rust/src/transmission.rs alongside . conventions, hard rules: Signature. Take agent Columns read/mutate, i32 count (active agents), i32 n_nodes, &Distributions. Take Columns & (read) &mut (write). Storage types. state u8, timer u16, nodeid u16. Get typed slice matching accessor: state.as_u8_mut(), timer.as_u16_mut(), nodeid.as_u16(), (as_u32/as_i32_mut/as_f64/…) dtypes. panic dtype mismatch. Always slice live prefix [..count]. Return per-node counts; touch census buffers. Return List integer[n_nodes] (named, e.g. list!(waned = …, onset = …)) single Vec<i32>. R caller applies move_count. Never write node census inside kernel. Parallelize per-node tallies. Use par_chunks_mut(rng::RNG_CHUNK), accumulate private vec![0i64; k * n] per chunk, reduce summing. shared writes. Randomness goes rng. Pull one let base = rng::next_call_base(); parallel fan-, let mut rng = rng::chunk_rng(base, chunk_index); inside chunk. makes seeded run reproducible independent thread count — use thread_rng() seed per-thread. Decrement guard. t[j] > 0 { t[j] -= 1; } testing == 0 (u16 must underflow), branch agent’s state just-arrived state advanced twice. Validate assert! (becomes R error). Register . Add fn your_kernel; file’s extendr_module! { … }, ensure file’s module listed src/rust/src/lib.rs (mod x; use x; inside extendr_module!). ’s new file, add lines.

title: Using and extending Razer: from R callbacks to custom Rust kernels
text: generic kernel flips agents from_state to_state per-agent probability p returns per-node counts — template new kernels start : …add mod myrule; use myrule; lib.rs, run devtools::document(), call callback, applying counts move_count: rng.gen::<f64>() rand’s Rng-trait method uniform draw [0, 1) (hence use rand::Rng;) — per-agent draw transmission.rs uses turn per-node probability infections.
code: // src/rust/src/myrule.rs use extendr_api::prelude::*; use rayon::prelude::*; use crate::column::Column; use crate::rng; use rand::Rng;                                              // for rng.gen::<f64>()  /// Flip each agent in `from_state` to `to_state` with probability `p`. /// /// @param state   Per-agent u8 state Column (mutated). /// @param nodeid  Per-agent u16 node-id Column. /// @param count   Number of active agents. /// @param n_nodes Number of nodes (length of the returned vector). /// @param from_state,to_state  u8 state codes (as i32). /// @param p       Per-agent flip probability in [0, 1]. /// @return Integer vector of per-node flip counts. /// @export #[extendr] fn flip_state(     state: &mut Column,     nodeid: &Column,     count: i32,     n_nodes: i32,     from_state: i32,     to_state: i32,     p: f64, ) -> Vec<i32> {     assert!(count >= 0 && n_nodes >= 0, "`count`/`n_nodes` must be non-negative");     assert!((0.0..=1.0).contains(&p), "`p` must be in [0, 1]");     let (count, n) = (count as usize, n_nodes as usize);     let (from, to) = (from_state as u8, to_state as u8);      let base = rng::next_call_base();                       // ONE base per call, before fan-out     let st  = &mut state.as_u8_mut()[..count];     let nid = &nodeid.as_u16()[..count];      let tally: Vec<i64> = st         .par_chunks_mut(rng::RNG_CHUNK)         .enumerate()         .zip(nid.par_chunks(rng::RNG_CHUNK))         .map(|((ci, s), node)| {             let mut rng = rng::chunk_rng(base, ci);          // deterministic per-chunk stream             let mut local = vec![0i64; n];             for j in 0..s.len() {                 if s[j] == from && rng.gen::<f64>() < p {    // rand's Rng::gen: uniform [0, 1)                     s[j] = to;                     local[node[j] as usize] += 1;                 }             }             local         })         .reduce(|| vec![0i64; n], |mut a, b| { for k in 0..n { a[k] += b[k]; } a });      tally.iter().map(|&x| x as i32).collect() }  extendr_module! {     mod myrule;     fn flip_state; } step_exit = function(model) {   flips <- flip_state(model$people$state, model$people$nodeid, model$people$count,                       length(model$states) - 1L, model$states[["S"]], model$states[["V"]], 0.01)   move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, flips, model$tick) }

what: 6. Prompting an LLM to write the Rust kernel
title: Using and extending Razer: from R callbacks to custom Rust kernels
text: LLM given conventions exemplar can write correct Razer kernel reliably. recipe: Give context: exemplar files verbatim: src/rust/src/steps.rs (ABI action) target file ’re adding . Tell style/ABI match. eight hard rules §5 (return per-node counts, u16 timers + decrement guard, rng::next_call_base + rng::chunk_rng per chunk thread-independent reproducibility, assert! validation, register file’s extendr_module! lib.rs). commenting convention: codebase read C/C++/C#/Python programmers know Rust — comment Rust-specific idioms (&/&mut, slices, match, closures, iterators, Option/Result/?, unsafe, #[extendr] generates). dev loop: devtools::document() devtools::test(); panics become R errors. copy-paste prompt template: adding kernel Razer, R package whose performance kernels Rust exposed R via #[extendr]. Follow existing ABI exactly — reference exemplar: Task: write kernel «name» , active agent, «describe per-agent computation precisely: Columns reads/writes, condition, state change, timer/distribution, per-node quantity count». Requirements (deviate): - Signature takes agent Columns, count: i32, n_nodes: i32, &Distributions; take Columns &/&mut; slice [..count]. - state u8, timer u16, nodeid u16; use as_u8_mut/as_u16_mut/ as_u16. Guard timer decrements (t>0 { t-=1 }). - Return per-node counts Vec<i32> named List integer[n_nodes]; write node census inside kernel. - Parallelize par_chunks_mut(rng::RNG_CHUNK) + private per-node Vec<i64> reduced summing. - randomness: let base = rng::next_call_base(); fan-, rng::chunk_rng(base, chunk_index) inside chunk (keeps seeded runs reproducible regardless thread count). Never use thread_rng. - Validate inputs assert!. - Add fn «name»; file’s extendr_module!, tell exact lib.rs edits. - Comment Rust idioms C/C#/Python reader (per repo convention). - Write roxygen/extendr doc comment @param/@return/@export. - Also write testthat test asserting returned per-node counts equal direct R census mutated agent states ~1e6 agents, plus reproducibility check set_seed(). paste back compiler errors devtools::document(), fix . Iterate pasting compiler/test output back LLM. panics become R errors test compares independent R census, get tight correctness loop.

title: Using and extending Razer: from R callbacks to custom Rust kernels
text: Collected hard rules (see §5 detail): Stay R unless task needs novel per-agent computation every tick isn’t composition step_timer_expire(_set) + move_count + vital/import/bincount kernels. wish write per-agent loop R, round-trip whole agent Column R tick, means: write Rust. Kernel ABI: #[extendr] fn, args = agent Columns + count: i32 + n_nodes: i32 + &Distributions; Columns &/&mut, sliced [..count]; state u8 / timer u16 / nodeid u16 via as_u8_mut/as_u16_mut/as_u16; return per-node counts (Vec<i32> named List), never touch node census; parallelize par_chunks_mut(rng::RNG_CHUNK) + per-chunk Vec<i64> reduced sum. RNG: rng::next_call_base() fan-, rng::chunk_rng(base, ci) per chunk — thread-count-independent reproducibility. Never thread_rng. Validate assert! (→ R error). Guard u16 timer decrements. Register file’s extendr_module! src/rust/src/lib.rs (mod/use). Build: devtools::document() (recompiles + regenerates wrappers/man), devtools::test(). Comment Rust idioms non-Rust (C/C#/Python) reader. Exemplars: src/rust/src/steps.rs, src/rust/src/transmission.rs. Conventions: CLAUDE.md.

code: N <- 1e6L; nticks <- 365L; D <- dist_gamma(140, 0.05)   # mean 7-day periods runs <- lapply(c("SIR", "SIRS", "SEIR", "SEIRS"), function(mod) {   args <- list(scenario = data.frame(population = N, I = 100L), model = mod,                nticks = nticks, r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = D, seed = 1L)   if (grepl("E", mod))            args$incubation_period <- D   if (mod %in% c("SIRS", "SEIRS")) args$immunity_period  <- 60   do.call(run_model, args) }) names(runs) <- c("SIR", "SIRS", "SEIR", "SEIRS")  col_comp  <- c(S = "#1f78b4", E = "#ff7f00", I = "#e31a1c", R = "#33a02c") lty_model <- c(SIR = 1, SIRS = 2, SEIR = 3, SEIRS = 5) plot(NA, xlim = c(0, nticks - 1), ylim = c(0, 1), xlab = "day", ylab = "agents (millions)",      main = "SIR / SIRS / SEIR / SEIRS") for (mod in names(runs)) for (cmp in c("S", "E", "I", "R")) {   col <- runs[[mod]]$nodes[[cmp]]   if (!is.null(col)) lines(0:(nticks - 1), rowSums(col$values()) / 1e6,                            col = col_comp[cmp], lty = lty_model[mod], lwd = 1.8) } legend("top", names(col_comp), col = col_comp, lwd = 2, lty = 1, horiz = TRUE, bty = "n") legend("right", names(lty_model), col = "grey30", lwd = 2, lty = lty_model, bty = "n")

what: 3. Add an intervention with a callback
title: Razer — getting started (R Notebook)
text: run_model() takes init / step_enter / step_update / step_exit callbacks extra_states argument user-defined states. one-vaccination moves 40% still-susceptible population new "V" state day 10 — early, wave takes — something prevent (see examples/sia_campaigns.R examples/quarantine.R scheduled / leaky versions). compare SIR model without pulse, difference intervention alone.
code: sir_args <- list(scenario, "SIR", nticks = 200L, r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = 8, seed = 1L) base <- do.call(run_model, sir_args)                       # no intervention vacc <- do.call(run_model, c(sir_args, list(extra_states = "V",                   step_exit = function(model) {                     if (model$tick != 10L) return(invisible())                     V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]                     s <- model$people$state$values()                     take <- which(s == S); take <- take[runif(length(take)) < 0.4]                     s[take] <- V; model$people$state$set(s)                     move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, length(take), model$tick)                   }))) matplot(0:199, cbind(`no intervention` = rowSums(base$nodes$I$values()),                      vaccinated = rowSums(vacc$nodes$I$values())) / 1e3,         type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = c("#e31a1c", "#1f78b4"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "infectious (thousands)",         main = "Same SIR, with vs without a day-10 vaccination pulse") legend("topright", c("no intervention", "day-10 pulse (40% of S -> V)"),        col = c("#e31a1c", "#1f78b4"), lwd = 2, bty = "n")

title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: Supplemental Immunization Activity: schedule, targeted nodes, move susceptible new "V" state probability coverage. V protected — force infection reads census, disease step never touches V. Three independent patches, two targeted one control, make node-targeting visible.
code: n_nodes <- 3L; pop <- 3e5L; nticks <- 3L * 365L campaign_ticks <- seq(30L, nticks - 2L, by = 365L)   # annual, first one early (preventive) affected <- c(0L, 1L); coverage <- 0.6  campaign <- function(model) {   if (!(model$tick %in% campaign_ticks)) return(invisible())   V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]   s <- model$people$state$values(); nid <- model$people$nodeid$values()   cnt <- model$people$count   elig <- which(s[seq_len(cnt)] == S & nid[seq_len(cnt)] %in% affected)   vacc <- elig[runif(length(elig)) < coverage]                # probabilistic coverage   if (!length(vacc)) return(invisible())   s[vacc] <- V; model$people$state$set(s)   move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, tabulate(nid[vacc] + 1L, model$nodes$count), model$tick) }  vax <- run_model(data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(50L, n_nodes)),                  "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = 2, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                  network = matrix(0, n_nodes, n_nodes), seed = 1L,                  extra_states = "V", step_exit = campaign)  yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365; col <- c("#1b9e77", "#7570b3", "#d95f02") par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) matplot(yr, vax$nodes$V$values() / 1e3, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = col,         xlab = "year", ylab = "vaccinated (000s)", main = "V staircases up in the two targeted nodes") legend("topleft", c("node 0 (targeted)", "node 1 (targeted)", "node 2 (control)"), col = col, lwd = 2, bty = "n") matplot(yr, vax$nodes$I$values() / 1e3, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = col,         xlab = "year", ylab = "infectious (000s)", main = "...and only the control burns through")

what: 2. Add waning immunity (V → S) — one extra callback
title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: Permanent immunity just absence return transition. make V wane, set timer vaccinating add step_update runs step_timer_expire(V → S) tick. ’s entire change — new kernel, disease-step edit.  Permanent V staircased ; waning V sawtooths — campaign code, plus one timer one step_update. composability point.
code: waning_days <- dist_normal(2 * 365, 90)   # ~2-year immunity, per-agent draw  campaign_timed <- function(model) {        # as before, but also set the waning timer   if (!(model$tick %in% campaign_ticks)) return(invisible())   V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]   s <- model$people$state$values(); nid <- model$people$nodeid$values(); tm <- model$people$timer$values()   cnt <- model$people$count   elig <- which(s[seq_len(cnt)] == S & nid[seq_len(cnt)] %in% affected)   vacc <- elig[runif(length(elig)) < coverage]   if (!length(vacc)) return(invisible())   s[vacc] <- V; tm[vacc] <- pmax(1L, round(waning_days$sample_n(length(vacc))))   model$people$state$set(s); model$people$timer$set(tm)   move_count(model$nodes$S, model$nodes$V, tabulate(nid[vacc] + 1L, model$nodes$count), model$tick) } wane <- function(model) {                  # the new piece: V -> S on timer expiry   V <- model$states[["V"]]; S <- model$states[["S"]]   w <- step_timer_expire(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                          model$people$count, model$nodes$count, V, S)   move_count(model$nodes$V, model$nodes$S, w, model$tick) } vaxw <- run_model(data.frame(population = rep(pop, n_nodes), I = rep(50L, n_nodes)),                   "SIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = 2, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4),                   network = matrix(0, n_nodes, n_nodes), seed = 1L, extra_states = "V",                   step_update = wane, step_exit = campaign_timed) par(mfrow = c(1, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) matplot(yr, vaxw$nodes$V$values() / 1e3, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = col,         xlab = "year", ylab = "vaccinated (000s)",         main = "With waning, V sawtooths (campaign jumps, then decay) — campaigns must repeat") legend("topleft", c("node 0", "node 1", "node 2 (control)"), col = col, lwd = 2, bty = "n")

what: 3. Quarantine (a “Q” state with a different timed transition)
title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: recipe gives case isolation. periodic, leaky testing programme detects infectious agent probability sensitivity moves → Q; isolated agents stop transmitting (Q isn’t census). fixed period step_update releases Q → R — step_timer_expire targets R instead S.
code: test_ticks <- seq(0L, 199L, by = 14L); sensitivity <- 0.8; iso_days <- 10L detect <- function(model) {   if (!(model$tick %in% test_ticks)) return(invisible())   I <- model$states[["I"]]; Q <- model$states[["Q"]]   s <- model$people$state$values(); nid <- model$people$nodeid$values(); cnt <- model$people$count   inf <- which(s[seq_len(cnt)] == I); det <- inf[runif(length(inf)) < sensitivity]   # 20% missed   if (!length(det)) return(invisible())   s[det] <- Q; tm <- model$people$timer$values(); tm[det] <- iso_days   model$people$state$set(s); model$people$timer$set(tm)   move_count(model$nodes$I, model$nodes$Q, tabulate(nid[det] + 1L, model$nodes$count), model$tick) } release <- function(model) {   Q <- model$states[["Q"]]; R <- model$states[["R"]]   r <- step_timer_expire(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                          model$people$count, model$nodes$count, Q, R)   move_count(model$nodes$Q, model$nodes$R, r, model$tick) } base <- run_model(data.frame(population = 5e5L, I = 100L), "SIR", nticks = 200L,                   r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4), seed = 1L) quar <- run_model(data.frame(population = 5e5L, I = 100L), "SIR", nticks = 200L,                   r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 4), seed = 1L,                   extra_states = "Q", step_update = release, step_exit = detect) matplot(0:199, cbind(baseline = rowSums(base$nodes$I$values()),                      quarantine = rowSums(quar$nodes$I$values())) / 1e3,         type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2.5, col = c("#d7301f", "#2c7fb8"),         xlab = "day", ylab = "infectious (000s)",         main = "Fortnightly 80%-sensitive case isolation flattens the curve") legend("topright", c("baseline", "with quarantine"), col = c("#d7301f", "#2c7fb8"), lwd = 2.5, bty = "n")

title: Interventions: custom states, vaccination campaigns, and quarantine
text: three ingredients — registered state, callback re-labels agents + move_counts census, step_timer_expire timed return — compose much : Stack interventions. Register c("V", "Q") together run vaccination isolation callbacks model. timed transitions. step_timer_expire(, ) works pair — treatment state T → R, hospitalised H → R/H → D, post-infection temporary immunity, etc. Targeting attributes. Add per-agent Column init (age, risk group, dob) select intervention recipients bincount_where() predicates (e.g. vaccinate -fives: dob > tick - 5*365). Reactive triggers. Make campaign fire incidence crosses threshold testing model$nodes$incidence inside step_enter instead using fixed schedule. Drivers. step_update runs just calc_foi, edit model$nodes$beta / model$network model behaviour change lockdowns affecting tick’s transmission (census edits, contrast, affect next tick — see ?run_model).

title: Long runs and memory: capacity and squash()
text: Razer agent-based — every individual occupies slot fixed-size Column arrays. closed population easy: allocate exactly NN slots. add births, array must hold every agent ever exist. century crude birth rate 30/1,000/year, tens millions slots even though million ever alive . Allocating cumulative total wasteful; fix reclaim slots dead reuse . Two helpers bracket choice: calc_capacity(birthrates, pop) — cumulative-births bound: many agents ever born (safety factor). Use never reclaim. calc_capacity_cdr(birthrates, deathrates, pop) — peak-living bound: net births minus conservatively -counted death rate. Use reclaim dead slots periodically squash(). squash(people) compacts living agents front every per-agent Column (keeping aligned) drops dead, returning new live count — active count tracks living population instead cumulative total.
code: N0 <- 500000L; years <- 100L; nticks <- years * 365L; cbr <- 30; cdr <- 15 br <- matrix(cbr, nticks - 1L, 1L); dr <- matrix(cdr, nticks - 1L, 1L) cap_cdr   <- as.integer(calc_capacity_cdr(br, dr, N0, safety_factor = 1))   # we allocate this cap_naive <- as.integer(min(calc_capacity(br, N0, safety_factor = 1), .Machine$integer.max)) cat(sprintf("calc_capacity_cdr (peak-living, with squash) = %s slots\n", format(cap_cdr, big.mark = ","))) ## calc_capacity_cdr (peak-living, with squash) = 4,564,671 slots cat(sprintf("calc_capacity     (cumulative, no reclaim)   = %s slots  (%.1fx more)\n",             format(cap_naive, big.mark = ","), cap_naive / cap_cdr)) ## calc_capacity     (cumulative, no reclaim)   = 42,128,784 slots  (9.2x more)

title: Long runs and memory: capacity and squash()
text: disease (model "SI" r0 = 0, nothing transmits) — just demography. Crude, age-independent mortality exponential lifespan CDR hazard (Kaplan–Meier table built exponential survival curve). Newborns enter maternal state M (registered via extra_states; run_model wanes M→S us). step_exit callback runs births mortality tick squash()es year, recording living active counts plots.  living population grows steadily, active slot count tracks closely — staying well allocated calc_capacity_cdr line far calc_capacity (-reclaim) bound. gap memory squash() saves: without allocate every agent ever born.
code: h_year <- -log(1 - cdr / 1000)                                  # constant per-year hazard km <- kaplan_meier_estimator(round((1 - exp(-h_year * (0:200))) * 1e7))   # exponential life table mat_dur <- dist_normal(270, 20); squash_every <- 365L  track <- new.env() track$live <- numeric(nticks); track$live[1] <- N0 track$active <- numeric(nticks); track$active[1] <- N0  m <- run_model(data.frame(population = N0), "SI", nticks = nticks, r0 = 0,                infectious_period = dist_constant(7),            # unused (r0 = 0)                capacity = cap_cdr, extra_states = "M", seed = 1L,                init = function(model) {                  cap <- model$people$capacity                  model$people$dob <- allocate_scalar("i32", cap)                  model$people$dod <- allocate_scalar("u32", cap)                  model$nodes$birth_rate <- values_map(cbr / 1000 / 365, nticks, 1L)                  age <- pmin(as.integer(floor(stats::rexp(N0, h_year / 365))), 90L * 365L)                  model$people$dob$set(c(-age, integer(cap - N0)))                  model$people$dod$set(c(as.integer(km$predict_age_at_death(age, -1L) - age),                                         integer(cap - N0)))                },                step_exit = function(model) {                  t <- model$tick; idx <- t + 2L                  b <- births(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                              model$people$dob, model$people$dod, model$people$count, 1L,                              model$nodes$birth_rate, mat_dur, km, t)                  model$people$count <- b$count; move_count(NULL, model$nodes$M, b$born, t)                  d <- mortality(model$people$state, model$people$dod, model$people$nodeid,                                 model$people$count, 1L, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$M, NULL, d$m, t); move_count(model$nodes$S, NULL, d$s, t)                  if ((t + 1L) %% squash_every == 0L) model$people$count <- squash(model$people)                  track$live[idx]   <- sum(model$nodes$M$values()[idx, ], model$nodes$S$values()[idx, ])                  track$active[idx] <- model$people$count                }) cat(sprintf("living %s -> %s (%.1fx growth); peak active = %s of %s capacity (%.0f%% used)\n",             format(N0, big.mark = ","), format(round(track$live[nticks]), big.mark = ","),             track$live[nticks] / N0, format(round(max(track$active)), big.mark = ","),             format(cap_cdr, big.mark = ","), 100 * max(track$active) / cap_cdr)) ## living 500,000 -> 1,915,754 (3.8x growth); peak active = 1,947,029 of 4,564,671 capacity (43% used) yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) plot(yr, track$live / 1e6, type = "l", lwd = 2.5, col = "#238b45", ylim = c(0, cap_naive / 1e6 * 1.05),      xlab = "year", ylab = "agents (millions)", main = "Living population vs the two capacity bounds") abline(h = cap_cdr / 1e6, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f") abline(h = cap_naive / 1e6, lty = 3, lwd = 2, col = "grey40") legend("topleft", c("living population", "calc_capacity_cdr (allocated)", "calc_capacity (no reclaim)"),        col = c("#238b45", "#d7301f", "grey40"), lwd = c(2.5, 2, 2), lty = c(1, 2, 3), bty = "n") matplot(yr, cbind(active = track$active, living = track$live) / 1e6, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2,         col = c("#2c7fb8", "#238b45"), ylim = c(0, cap_cdr / 1e6 * 1.05),         xlab = "year", ylab = "agents (millions)", main = "Annual squash() keeps active slots near the living count") abline(h = cap_cdr / 1e6, lty = 2, lwd = 2, col = "#d7301f") legend("topleft", c("active slots (people$count)", "living population", "allocated capacity"),        col = c("#2c7fb8", "#238b45", "#d7301f"), lwd = c(2, 2, 2), lty = c(1, 1, 2), bty = "n")

title: Long runs and memory: capacity and squash()
text: Squash cadence. Squash less often (e.g. every 5 years) active count sawtooths higher reclaims (dead slots accumulate, drop) — trading little head-room fewer compactions. Size capacity worst-case inter-squash peak. Safety factor. calc_capacity_cdr(..., safety_factor = s) credits 1/(1+s) deaths, larger s reserves slack lower-mortality (faster-growing) realization; s = 0 bare net-growth bound. Add disease. Swap "SI", r0 = 0 real model incubation_period / transmission endemic, demographically open simulation can run centuries within bounded array (exactly long measles polio run needs).

title: Spatial epidemics: metapopulations and migration networks
text: single well-mixed population useful fiction, real epidemics play across metapopulation — set patches (towns, districts, countries) local transmission, coupled movement people. Coupling drives phenomena single-patch model can’t show: spatial invasion waves, rescue faded-patch neighbours, partial synchrony patches. Razer models metapopulation nn patches running usual SIR transmission, plus coupling matrix WW WijW_{ij} fraction patch ii’s force infection exported patch jj. kernel calc_foi redistributes tick: λk=rk(1−∑jWkj)+∑iriWik,rk=βkIkNk,\lambda_k \;=\; r_k\Big(1 - \textstyle\sum_j W_{kj}\Big) \;+\; \sum_i r_i\,W_{ik}, \qquad r_k = \beta_k\,\frac{I_k}{N_k}, .e. patch kk keeps un-exported part local force imports share every patch’s.

title: Spatial epidemics: metapopulations and migration networks
text: use England & Wales measles patches (954 registration districts, 1944 population latitude/longitude). Razer ships several migration models turn pairwise distances + populations weight matrix — gravity(), radiation(), stouffer(), competing_destinations() — plus row_normalizer() cap patch’s total emigration rows valid export fractions. : parameter-free radiation model (Simini et al., Nature 2012), capped 10% emigration per patch.
code: # A pkgdown article under vignettes/articles/, so the shared example data is two up. scenario <- read.csv(file.path("..", "..", "examples", "data", "EnglandWalesMeasles_places.csv")) D  <- distances(scenario$latitude, scenario$longitude)            # pairwise great-circle km W  <- radiation(scenario$population, D, k = 1, include_home = FALSE) W  <- row_normalizer(W, max_rowsum = 0.1)                         # cap total emigration at 10% cat(sprintf("%d patches; total population %s; network row-sums in [%.3f, %.3f]\n",             nrow(scenario), format(sum(scenario$population), big.mark = ","),             min(rowSums(W)), max(rowSums(W)))) ## 954 patches; total population 30,182,538; network row-sums in [0.009, 0.100]

title: Spatial epidemics: metapopulations and migration networks
text: run_model() takes coupling matrix directly network; internally stored 2-D Column calc_foi reads Rust-side tick. seed infectious ~5% immune every patch run daily SIR ~4 months.
code: scenario$I <- pmin(5L, scenario$population)                       # 5 infectious / patch scenario$R <- pmin(as.integer(0.05 * scenario$population), scenario$population - scenario$I) m <- run_model(scenario, "SIR", nticks = 120L, r0 = 2,                infectious_period = dist_gamma(2, 2), network = W, seed = 1L)  days <- 0:119 S <- rowSums(m$nodes$S$values()); I <- rowSums(m$nodes$I$values()); R <- rowSums(m$nodes$R$values()) matplot(days, cbind(S, I, R) / 1e6, type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2.5,         col = c("#2c7fb8", "#d7301f", "#238b45"), xlab = "day", ylab = "agents (millions)",         main = "National S / I / R, summed over 954 patches") legend("right", c("S", "I", "R"), col = c("#2c7fb8", "#d7301f", "#238b45"), lwd = 2.5, bty = "n")

title: Spatial epidemics: metapopulations and migration networks
text: model spatial, interesting output per patch. patch’s census column m$nodes$$values(); map attack rate (fraction ever infected) patch’s coordinates, sized population — recognizable shape England & Wales.
code: attack <- colSums(m$nodes$incidence$values()) / scenario$population   # per-patch fraction infected pal <- grDevices::colorRampPalette(c("#ffffb2", "#fd8d3c", "#bd0026"))(64) rng <- range(attack); idx <- pmax(1L, pmin(64L, 1L + round(63 * (attack - rng[1]) / max(diff(rng), 1e-9)))) plot(scenario$longitude, scenario$latitude, pch = 19, asp = 1, col = pal[idx],      cex = 0.3 + 1.8 * sqrt(scenario$population / max(scenario$population)),      xlab = "longitude", ylab = "latitude", main = "Attack rate by patch (size ~ population)") lv <- pretty(rng, 4) legend("topleft", title = "attack rate", bty = "n", pch = 19, pt.cex = 1.4,        legend = sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * lv),        col = pal[pmax(1L, pmin(64L, 1L + round(63 * (lv - rng[1]) / max(diff(rng), 1e-9))))])

Seed single patch (e.g. largest) set R = 0 everywhere, map per-patch day peak (apply(I_mat, 2, .max)): patches light later farther sit seed, along network. Endemic + spatial. Add constant-population vitals step_exit callback endemic notebook (capacity imports) keep patches endemic study spatial persistence / rescue effects critical community size. Seasonality per patch. run_model’s seasonality accepts full n_ticks x n_nodes grid (via values_map), different patches can offset school terms climates.

title: Vital dynamics and maternal immunity: a measles model
text: Measles canonical childhood infection: transmissible (R0≈12R_0 \approx 12–1818), fully immunizing, — crucially — sustained birth new susceptibles. Modelling well needs SIR: M — maternal immunity: newborns temporarily protected maternal antibodies, wane S ~9 months. disease chain M → S → E → → R. vital dynamics: crude birth rate feeds newborns M, every agent realistic age date death natural mortality retires schedule. susceptible supply (births) makes system endemic, damped/forced recurrent epidemics familiar pre-vaccination measles.

title: Vital dynamics and maternal immunity: a measles model
text: Razer’s extensibility pays — kernel changes, just run_model() knobs: extra_states = "M" registers maternal state. disease kernels lead M→S waning leg, run_model applies us tick (recording waning_m flow). capacity (calc_capacity()) reserves slots population grow 20-year run; births kernel activates . init callback gives agent age (pyramid-like curve) Kaplan–Meier date death (see demographics notebook). step_exit callback runs births (newborns M, maternal timer + KM date death) mortality (retire agents whose dod arrived) tick.  Susceptibles hover near herd-immunity threshold N/R0N/R_0 (small 1/14 ≈ 7% reservoir births top ), M thin layer protected newborns, incidence breaks recurrent epidemics birth-fed susceptible pool repeatedly crosses threshold.
code: # A survivorship curve l(a) from a Gompertz–Makeham hazard; its complement (cumulative # deaths) is the life table, and l(a) itself is the initial age distribution. Deriving both # from one curve keeps the age structure and mortality mutually consistent. max_age <- 100L; ay <- 0:max_age haz <- pmin(0.0004 + 1e-5 * exp(0.115 * ay), 1); haz[1] <- 0.02 surv <- numeric(max_age + 2L); surv[1] <- 1 for (a in seq_len(max_age + 1L)) surv[a + 1L] <- surv[a] * (1 - haz[a]) surv[max_age + 2L] <- 0 cohort <- 1e6 age_dist <- aliased_distribution(round(surv[1:(max_age + 1L)] * cohort))      # initial ages km       <- kaplan_meier_estimator(round((surv[1] - surv[2:(max_age + 2L)]) * cohort))  # life table r0 <- 14; cbr <- 30; nticks <- 20L * 365L; pop <- 1000000L inf_dur <- dist_normal(7, 1.5); inc_dur <- dist_normal(7, 1.5)     # infectious / latent (days) mat_dur <- dist_normal(270, 20)                                   # maternal-immunity waning (~9 mo) capacity <- as.integer(calc_capacity(matrix(cbr, nticks - 1L, 1L), pop, safety_factor = 1))  # Seed near the herd-immunity threshold: immune fraction 1 - 1/R0, a small infectious spark. scenario <- data.frame(population = pop, R = round((1 - 1 / r0) * pop), I = 100L)  m <- run_model(scenario, "SEIR", nticks = nticks, r0 = r0, infectious_period = inf_dur,                incubation_period = inc_dur, capacity = capacity, extra_states = "M",                seed = 1L, progress = FALSE,                init = function(model) {                  cap <- model$people$capacity                  model$people$dob <- allocate_scalar("i32", cap)                  model$people$dod <- allocate_scalar("u32", cap)                  model$nodes$birth_rate <- values_map(cbr / 1000 / 365, nticks, 1L)                  model$nodes$births     <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, 1L)                  model$nodes$deaths     <- allocate_vector("i32", nticks - 1L, 1L)                  age_days <- age_dist$sample_n(pop) * 365L + as.integer(floor(stats::runif(pop) * 365))                  model$people$dob$set(c(-age_days, integer(cap - pop)))                  model$people$dod$set(c(as.integer(km$predict_age_at_death(age_days, -1L) - age_days),                                         integer(cap - pop)))                },                step_exit = function(model) {                  t <- model$tick                  b <- births(model$people$state, model$people$timer, model$people$nodeid,                              model$people$dob, model$people$dod, model$people$count, 1L,                              model$nodes$birth_rate, mat_dur, km, t)                  model$people$count <- b$count                  move_count(NULL, model$nodes$M, b$born, t); model$nodes$births$set_col(t, b$born)                  d <- mortality(model$people$state, model$people$dod, model$people$nodeid,                                 model$people$count, 1L, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$M, NULL, d$m, t); move_count(model$nodes$S, NULL, d$s, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$E, NULL, d$e, t); move_count(model$nodes$I, NULL, d$i, t)                  move_count(model$nodes$R, NULL, d$r, t); model$nodes$deaths$set_col(t, d$m+d$s+d$e+d$i+d$r)                }) cat(sprintf("living %s -> %s over 20 yr; cases %s; births %s; deaths %s\n",             format(pop, big.mark = ","),             format(round(sum(sapply(c("M","S","E","I","R"), function(s) m$nodes[[s]]$values()[nticks, ]))), big.mark = ","),             format(sum(m$nodes$incidence$values()), big.mark = ","),             format(sum(m$nodes$births$values()), big.mark = ","),             format(sum(m$nodes$deaths$values()), big.mark = ","))) ## living 1,000,000 -> 1,430,070 over 20 yr; cases 641,290; births 715,584; deaths 285,514 yr <- (seq_len(nticks) - 1L) / 365 S <- rowSums(m$nodes$S$values()); R <- rowSums(m$nodes$R$values()); M <- rowSums(m$nodes$M$values()) par(mfrow = c(2, 1), mar = c(4, 4.5, 2.5, 1)) matplot(yr, cbind(S, R, M), type = "l", lty = 1, lwd = 2, col = c("steelblue", "darkgreen", "purple"),         xlab = "year", ylab = "agents", main = "Immune-status reservoirs: S, R, M") abline(h = pop / r0, lty = 3, col = "steelblue")                 # S* = N / R0 legend("right", c("S", "R", "M", "N/R0"), col = c("steelblue", "darkgreen", "purple", "steelblue"),        lwd = c(2, 2, 2, 1), lty = c(1, 1, 1, 3), bty = "n") plot(yr[-1], rowSums(m$nodes$incidence$values()), type = "l", col = "firebrick",      xlab = "year", ylab = "new infections / day", main = "Daily incidence: recurrent measles epidemics") abline(v = 0:20, col = "grey92")

title: Vital dynamics and maternal immunity: a measles model
text: Vaccination. Layer SIA campaign top — register c("M", "V") add campaign callback (see interventions notebook); watch inter-epidemic period lengthen coverage rises. Transmissibility / demography. Change r0, cbr, maternal/latent/infectious distributions; higher birth rates shorten inter-epidemic period (faster susceptible replenishment). Spatial measles. Give scenario multiple patches network (spatial notebook) study classic city-size / fade---rescue measles dynamics. Memory long runs. decades array fills dead; reclaim squash() size calc_capacity_cdr() instead calc_capacity() — long-runs notebook.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Rust-backed Agent modeling Zero-copy typed arrays Eradication Research Razer R interface LASER (Light Agent Spatial modeling ERadication) toolkit. provides high-performance, Rust-backed data structures composable kernels large-scale spatial agent-based disease models. name deliberate pun: “raze” means eradicate completely, “razor” echoes LASER, trailing R anchors R.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: LASER Python framework developed Institute Disease Modeling building fast, composable agent-based disease models national global scale. core design insight struct--arrays (SoA) memory layout: instead one object per agent, agent property flat array, active agents contiguous slice array — cache-friendly vectorized, Numba-JIT-compiled kernels drive LASER’s performance. Razer ports memory model R. agent property Rust-owned, dtype-tagged array — Column — R holds opaque external-pointer handle (via extendr framework). per-tick simulation kernels borrow arrays directly mutate place copies, splitting per-agent work across CPU cores Rayon. Data crosses back R explicit inspection ($values()).

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Full models assembled run_model() () — composed hand per-tick kernels. See worked articles examples/.
code: library(razer)  # Disease-state codes shared by all kernels: c(S = 0, E = 1, I = 2, R = 3, M = 4, D = -1) states <- laser_states()  # A typed, Rust-owned agent-property array: one million u8 disease states. state  <- allocate_scalar("u8",  1e6)        # per-agent state (1 byte each) nodeid <- allocate_scalar("u16", 1e6)        # 0-based patch id per agent timer  <- allocate_scalar("u8",  1e6)        # state-timer countdown state$set(rep(states[["S"]], 1e6))           # write the whole buffer state$values()[1:5]                          # copy a snapshot back to R  # A per-node, per-tick report buffer (n_ticks x n_nodes), stored time-major so each # tick's per-node row is contiguous. The census is maintained INCREMENTALLY: each tick # carries column t forward to t+1, then kernels apply only their deltas. S <- allocate_vector("i32", 365L, 10L)       # 365 ticks x 10 nodes S$set_col(0L, rep(1e5, 10L))                 # tick 0, per-node susceptibles  # Draw state-timer durations from a parameterized distribution. inf_period <- dist_gamma(2, 4)               # mean 8 ticks inf_period$sample_n(5L)

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Column — Rust-owned, dtype-tagged array (i8/u8/i16/u16/i32/u32/f32/f64), allocated 1-D per-agent array (allocate_scalar) 2-D time × node report buffer (allocate_vector). $set()/$values()/$col()/$set_col() move data R; kernels operate buffer place. narrow integer widths (e.g. u8 state, u16 node id / timer) keep national-scale populations compact. State codes — laser_states() returns c(S, E, , R, M, D); M maternal immunity, D deceased (stored 255 u8 state column). Distributions — Distribution (dist_normal(), dist_gamma(), dist_constant(), …) supplies per-agent state-timer durations. Per-tick kernels — calc_foi (force infection, spatial coupling); transmission kernels transmission (S→E S→, sets u16 timer) transmission_si (S→absorbing); three step kernels step_si / step_sir / step_sirs (timed M→S / E→/ →{S,R} / R→S transitions SI…SEIRS menagerie, single u16-timer pass); carry_forward / carry_forward_states (incremental census) move_count (apply kernel’s returned counts); vital-dynamics kernels births, mortality, constant_pop_vitals_sir, import_infections. agent-loop kernels return per-node counts model applies census — model allocates states . parallelize per-agent work across cores private per-node accumulators. Demographics & initialization — AliasedDistribution (aliased_distribution) sampling ages pyramid, KaplanMeierEstimator (kaplan_meier_estimator) realistic dates death, load_pyramid_csv / sample_pyramid_ages, calc_capacity / calc_capacity_cdr sizing agent arrays births (latter bounds peak living population runs reclaim dead slots squash). Compaction & binning — squash reclaims slots departed (e.g. deceased) agents; parallel binning family bincount / bincount_wt (weighted) / bincount_where (predicate-filtered, count-aware) / bincount_where_wt (weighted + filtered) rolls per-agent properties per-node, per-tick reports. Spatial coupling — distances plus migration-network models (gravity, radiation, stouffer, competing_destinations, row_normalizer) build coupling matrix calc_foi redistributes force infection .

what: Modeling: per-tick ordering and the effective R0
title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Every model SI/SEI/SIS/SEIS/SIR/SEIR/SIRS/SEIRS menagerie transmission kernel (transmission S→E S→; transmission_si SI’s absorbing S→) plus one step kernel (step_si, step_sir absorbing state parameterized S R, step_sirs). step kernel leads M→S, model can add maternal state. Every model uses one per-tick ordering: carry_forward_states → step_* → calc_foi → transmission calc_foi placed immediately transmission (step kernel ). calc_foi reads settled start--interval infectious census [t] (working column building), value depend step kernel runs. newly infectious agent enters one census column infection recovers D columns later, contributing force infection exactly D columns — effective basic reproduction number full R0 = beta * D (never beta * (D − 1)), direct-S→E-entry families, special-casing. step kernel single pass branching agent’s entry state, just-entered timed state never decremented tick. See CLAUDE.md full rationale per-model table. closed-population menagerie usually don’t wire hand: run_model(scenario, model, nticks, r0, …) builds population, runs correct per-tick loop applies census deltas, returns model environment (bundling $people, $nodes, $network, $carry) per-node census incidence / onset / recovery / waning flows — e.g. run_model(data.frame(population = 1e5, = 100), "SEIR", nticks = 200, r0 = 2.5, infectious_period = 8, incubation_period = 5, seed = 1). seeds model’s E//R states scenario supplies, takes optional init plus per-tick callbacks step_enter (start) / step_update (step kernel calc_foi) / step_exit (end) extension (see examples/model_callbacks.R). examples articles show run_model() action go beyond menagerie vital dynamics, importation, maternal immunity.

title: Light Agent Spatial Modeling for Eradication (R)
text: Every model ships runnable script examples/ (Rscript examples/<name>.R; plots written examples/output/) paired, annotated teaching article package website. article walks epidemiology, Razer code builds model, customize extend : Getting started — run_model() workflow, model comparison, callback-based intervention. Epidemic final size — validating simulated attack fraction Kermack–McKendrick relation = 1 − exp(−R0·) (matched within thousandths across supercritical range, R0 = beta · D). Endemic dynamics — S*/N = 1/R0 equilibrium, importation, seasonal forcing. Spatial metapopulations — migration networks spatial attack-rate map England & Wales. Demographics — realistic age pyramids Kaplan–Meier dates death. Vital dynamics & measles — births, natural mortality, maternal-immunity M state full M-S-E--R model. Interventions — custom states callbacks: vaccination campaigns (± waning) quarantine. Long runs & memory — capacity sizing reclaiming dead slots squash() century. See examples/README.md full script list tips running RStudio. Customizing extending: Using extending Razer guide (written human modelers coding assistants) covers customizing purely R via callbacks, line ’s doable R calls new Rust kernel, set Rust development, copy-paste recipe prompting LLM write correct kernel.

what: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
text: Projects node's population forward (possibly time-varying) crude birth rate returns per-node capacity preallocate, inflated optional safety factor. births treated geometric growth: daily growth rate lambda = (1 + CBR/1000)^(1/365) - 1 summed time steps exponentiated (exp(sum lambda)), geometric-Brownian-motion-style estimate expected growth. safety factor adds multiple growth's standard-deviation-like term sqrt(exp(sum lambda)) - 1 headroom stochastic variation.

title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
code: calc_capacity(birthrates, initial_pop, safety_factor = 1)

title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
text: birthrates 2-D nsteps x nnodes numeric matrix crude birth rates (births per 1,000 individuals per year), 2-D razer Column (e.g. values_map()) whose $values() matrix. value must [0, 100]. initial_pop numeric vector length nnodes: initial population per node. Must non-negative. safety_factor Non-negative headroom multiplier [0, 6] (default 1). 0 gives bare expected-growth estimate; larger values reserve slack.

title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
text: numeric vector length nnodes estimated capacities (whole-valued doubles, represent integers exactly 2^53). warning issued estimate exceeds .Machine$integer.max (R's 32-bit signed integer max) — largest count razer's allocators (allocate_scalar/allocate_vector, whose count i32) can accept.

title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
text: Stops birthrates 2-D, node count match length(initial_pop), population negative, birth rate outside [0, 100], safety_factor outside [0, 6].

title: Estimate the agent capacity needed for a growing population. — calc_capacity
code: # two nodes, one year of a constant CBR of 40 per 1,000 br <- matrix(40, nrow = 365, ncol = 2) calc_capacity(br, initial_pop = c(1e6, 5e5), safety_factor = 1) #> [1] 1060599  530300

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
text: mortality-aware companion calc_capacity(). dead agents' slots reclaimed periodically squash(), slots needed bounded peak simultaneous living population — net births minus deaths — cumulative number ever born (calc_capacity() estimates -reclaim case). lets model decades centuries without allocating one slot per agent ever born.

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
code: calc_capacity_cdr(birthrates, deathrates, initial_pop, safety_factor = 1)

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
text: birthrates 2-D nsteps x nnodes matrix crude birth rates (per 1,000 per year), 2-D Column (e.g. values_map()). value [0, 100]. deathrates 2-D nsteps x nnodes matrix (2-D Column) crude death rates, shape birthrates. value [0, 100]. initial_pop non-negative numeric vector length nnodes: initial population. safety_factor Non-negative headroom multiplier [0, 6] (default 1), controlling much death rate underestimated. 0 credits deaths fully.

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
text: numeric vector length nnodes estimated capacities (whole-valued doubles). warning issued estimate exceeds .Machine$integer.max.

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
text: per-node daily birth death rates lambda = (1 + rate/1000)^(1/365) - 1 summed time steps; expected net-growth factor exp(sum lambda_b - sum lambda_d). conservative bound death rate underestimated safety factor — fraction 1 / (1 + safety_factor) death sum credited, holding rest back headroom lower-mortality (faster-growing) realization. safety_factor = 0 credits deaths fully (tightest, bare net-growth estimate); larger values credit fewer deaths reserve slack. (Unlike calc_capacity(), gross- births term enters — defeat point reclaiming slots.)

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
text: Stops either rate grid 2-D, shapes node counts disagree length(initial_pop), population negative, rate outside [0, 100], safety_factor outside [0, 6].

title: Estimate the agent capacity for a growing population reclaimed with squash(). — calc_capacity_cdr
code: # one node, 100 years, CBR 30 / CDR 15 — peak-living bound for a squash-reclaimed run br <- matrix(30, nrow = 100 * 365, ncol = 1) dr <- matrix(15, nrow = 100 * 365, ncol = 1) calc_capacity_cdr(br, dr, initial_pop = 1e6, safety_factor = 1) #> [1] 9129894

what: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. — calc_foi
title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. — calc_foi
text: Computes frequency-dependent, network-redistributed FOI rate column tick foi. local rate per node r[k] = beta[k] * seasonality[k] * infected[k] / population[k], redistributed foi[k] = r[k] * (1 - sum_j W[k, j]) + sum_i r[] * W[, k]. transmission kernels turn rate per-tick probability 1 - exp(-foi).

title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. — calc_foi
code: calc_foi(infected, population, beta, seasonality, network, foi, tick)

title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. — calc_foi
text: infected Infectious-count census Column (nodes$), slice_len == n_nodes. population Per-node population census Column (nodes$N); FOI denominator. beta Transmission-coefficient grid (n_ticks x n_nodes, values_map()). seasonality Seasonal-modifier grid (n_ticks x n_nodes, values_map()). network n_nodes x n_nodes coupling weights, column-major. Either R numeric matrix, razer Column n_nodes * n_nodes f64 holding matrix column-major order. Column form avoids re-marshalling matrix R every tick (build , e.g. nc <- allocate_vector("f64", n, n); nc$set(.vector(W))). foi 2-D f64 Column ((n_ticks-1) x n_nodes); column tick overwritten. tick 0-based tick index: reads beta[tick]/seasonality[tick] infected[tick]/population[tick] (start--interval census), writes foi[tick].

title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. — calc_foi
text: NULL (invisibly); result written foi.

title: Compute the per-node force of infection (FOI) for one tick. — calc_foi
text: Index conventions: infected population census buffers read start--interval column tick (settled census recorded tick tick, infectious count population start interval tick → tick+1); beta seasonality exogenous modifier grids read interval column tick; result written foi[tick]. Ordering effective infectious period. calc_foi reads SETTLED census column tick — working column tick+1 interval's step kernel transmission build — result depend step kernel runs. lets every model use ONE ordering: carry_forward → step → calc_foi → transmission, calc_foi placed immediately transmission. agent contributes FOI exactly D census columns occupies (enters column entry+1 via either step kernel's E→transmission's S→, recovers D columns later), realized basic reproduction number full R0 = beta * D — direct S→(SIR) SEIR-style entry, beta*(D-1) artifact per-family special-casing.

what: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
text: Builds agent population per-node scenario, seeds , advances one eight SI / SEI / SIS / SEIS / SIR / SEIR / SIRS / SEIRS models nticks ticks using Column kernels correct order (realized basic reproduction number full R0 = beta * D). tick runs, uniformly every model, carry_forward → step → calc_foi → transmission, calc_foi placed immediately transmission (step kernel ); run_model applies every census delta .

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
code: run_model(   scenario,   model,   nticks,   r0,   infectious_period,   incubation_period = NULL,   immunity_period = NULL,   network = NULL,   seasonality = 1,   seed = NULL,   progress = FALSE,   capacity = NULL,   extra_states = NULL,   init = NULL,   step_enter = NULL,   step_update = NULL,   step_exit = NULL )

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
text: scenario data.frame one row per node integer population column. Optional integer E / / R columns seed state per node — states model actually (e.g. E column ignored SIR). S remainder. seeded E + + R must exceed node's population. model One "SI", "SEI", "SIS", "SEIS", "SIR", "SEIR", "SIRS", "SEIRS" (case-insensitive). nticks Number recorded daily states (dynamics run nticks - 1 times). r0 Basic reproduction number; beta = r0 / mean(infectious_period). infectious_period Infectious period ticks (Distribution number). incubation_period Latent/exposed period (required SE* models). immunity_period Waning-immunity period (required SIRS / SEIRS). network Optional n_nodes x n_nodes spatial-coupling matrix (default: -zero matrix — independent nodes). seasonality Transmission modifier values_map()-broadcastable form (default 1). seed Optional integer seed; supplied, set_seed() called reproducible run. progress TRUE, draw text progress bar. capacity Optional agent-array capacity (>= total initial population, default). Reserve extra slots — e.g. calc_capacity() / calc_capacity_cdr() — callback can grow population births import_infections (activate reserved slots). default capacity, population closed (growth). extra_states Optional character vector extra agent-state names beyond model's S/E//R. name already laser_states() (e.g. "M") keeps code; NEW name (e.g. "V" vaccinated) assigned next free code, becoming genuine new agent state. gets census Column carried forward, totalled N, seeded tick 0 agents' states; assigned codes exposed model$states. disease kernels branch S/E//R/M, agent NEW state left untouched (infected, transitioned) — transitions drive callbacks (move S→V vaccinate; waning, set timer run step_timer_expire(V, S) step_update). "M" , run_model additionally applies step kernels' built-M→S waning tick (recording waning_m flow). init Optional function(model) called model built loop. Use add per-agent property Columns, add state census Column (append model$carry carried forward), set agent states/timers, etc. initial per-node census (re)derived agents' states init runs, changing agent states init keeps census consistent. step_enter Optional function(model) called start every tick (carry-forward). Receives model environment; model$tick current 0-based interval index. step_update Optional function(model) called every tick step kernel (disease-state progression) calc_foi. calc_foi reads settled start--interval census [t] (slice model$tick), edit FOI drivers — model$nodes$beta, model$nodes$seasonality, model$network (read slice t) — influence CURRENT tick's force infection. Census edits made land working slice t+1, affect NEXT tick's FOI, one. step_exit Optional function(model) called end every tick (transmission). Receives model environment (model$tick set).

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
text: Invisibly, model environment, : $people — agent arrays (state, nodeid, timer, plus count/capacity). $nodes — per-tick census Columns (S, E//R applicable, extra_states M), N, foi, per-interval flows: incidence (new infections, models), onset (E→, SE* models), recovery (S R, models -exit), waning (R→S, SIRS/SEIRS), waning_m (M→S, M extra state) — n_nodes-wide, time-major Column. $network — coupling weights 2-D f64 Column (n_nodes x n_nodes, column-major), built network matrix read calc_foi tick. $carry — list census Columns carried forward tick. $states — state-name→code map (laser_states() plus extra_states). $tick — loop, current 0-based interval index (callbacks).

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
text: people, nodes, network, carry objects bundled single model environment, also optional callbacks receive returned.

title: Run a closed-population agent-based model. — run_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ scenario <- data.frame(population = 1e5L, I = 100L) m <- run_model(scenario, "SEIR", nticks = 200L, r0 = 2.5,                infectious_period = 8, incubation_period = 5, seed = 1) tail(m$nodes$I$values()) } # }
