title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: document compares two living codebases: LASER — Institute Disease Modeling’s Python toolkit fast, composable, spatial agent-based disease models. Two layers matter : laser-core (performance substrate: LaserFrame struct--arrays container, PropertySet, distance/migration models, demographic samplers, Kaplan–Meier estimator, RNG, Numba-compiled kernels) laser-generic (component library assembles SI/SIR/SEIR-family models reusable processes). Razer — R package repository, extendr/Rust port architecture. Razer side every claim grounded repository’s source. LASER side grounded documented architecture (software-overview tutorial docs) established public design; exact Python API signature matter verify source tree, describe behavior rather invent signature. Treat LASER specifics architecturally accurate line-cited.

what: 1. What LASER is, and why it is fast
title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: LASER’s core insight struct--arrays (SoA) memory layout. Instead one Python object per agent, every agent property flat array active population contiguous prefix array. LaserFrame: capacity-sized set parallel NumPy arrays (state, nodeid, timers, dates birth/death, …) plus live count, helpers add scalar/vector properties, grow population reserved capacity, squash dead slots can reused. second LaserFrame typically holds per-node / per-patch report time series. Performance comes two-tool stack: NumPy supplies typed, contiguous arrays vectorized bulk operations (reductions, masked assignment, slicing) — cache-friendly implemented C. Numba JIT-compiles hot per-agent loops — transmission, timer decrements, births, mortality — native code, @njit(parallel=True) prange giving thread-level parallelism agent array. laser-generic layers component model top: Model owns people patches LaserFrames PropertySet parameters, runs ordered list components (e.g. Births, Mortality, Susceptibility, Transmission, exposed/infectious/recovered progressions) per tick. component class initialization per-tick step behavior; model composed choosing components register. New science = new component class, core changes. whole thing Python-native: arrays NumPy arrays user can slice, plot, analyze directly; parameters attribute-accessible; components introspectable objects.

what: 2. The R problem: no NumPy, no Numba
title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: R replicate stack directly, two structural reasons: NumPy equivalent. Base R vectors limited logical, integer (32-bit signed), double, complex — unsigned narrow integer types (u8/u16/u32), matter enormously national/global agent counts (u8 state + u16 node id + u16 timer 5 bytes/agent; R doubles 24). Worse, R copy--modify semantics: mutating array function generally copies . -place, mutable, typed buffer hot loops can hammer without allocation. Numba equivalent. R production JIT tight numerical loops. byte-code compiler approach native speed, per-agent loop millions agents interpreted R orders magnitude slow. R’s performance story “push loop C/Fortran/vectorized primitives,” “compile loop.” faithful port pure R. performance-critical layer live compiled language.

title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: Razer fills gaps Rust, exposed R extendr: defining trade-Column approach: agent data R-visible array. lives Rust crosses R explicit $values() snapshot. buys real wins — copy--modify, narrow dtypes R can’t express, zero-copy kernel access — cost easy, interactive NumPy-style poking LASER users enjoy. Rust-vs-Numba , balance, favorable swap: kernels compiled install (per-session JIT warm-), borrow checker makes -place mutation safe, Rayon’s work-stealing parallelism mature. cost writing new kernel requires writing Rust — -language escape hatch comparable “just write another @njit function Python.”

what: 4.1 Data structures — equivalent in spirit, different in ergonomics

title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: component framework. Extensibility callbacks + Rust, first-class plugins. open-ended composability laser-generic’s whole point absent. Opaque data. Agent arrays aren’t R-visible; interactive, “just np.” ergonomics lost. R users expecting data-frame-like fluency feel friction. Extending fast paths requires Rust. pure-R modeler hits wall moment need novel per-agent kernel; -language JIT fall back . Missing infrastructure: PropertySet, snapshots, SortedQueue/event queue, within-host age-structured-contact machinery, fixed model set. Maturity bus factor. Razer 0.0.0.9000, single-author, pre-release. LASER established, multi-contributor, actively developed toolkit.

what: 6. Changes to give Razer parity with LASER
title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: Roughly priority order: component abstraction. registry process objects (init/step/report hooks) model environment convert callback escape hatch real, third-party-extensible framework — single biggest gap. PropertySet equivalent — structured, serializable parameter object provenance sweeps. Snapshot save/load checkpoint/restart long runs. R-friendly view layer Columns (lazy accessors, .data.frame, maybe ALTREP-backed read-view) recover NumPy-style ergonomics without copies. dynamics: within-host, age-structured contact matrices, broader transmission options, event/queue mechanism (SortedQueue). documented Rust extension path (constrained DSL) adding kernel supported workflow, fork. real head--head benchmark LASER replace design-level “comparable” claims numbers.

what: 7. Decisions in Razer that should be backported to LASER
title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: Stated plainly, places port improved original: Thread-count-independent reproducibility. Seeding fixed-size agent chunks (call_base, chunk_index) instead relying global RNG prange gives runs reproduce regardless core count. correctness/credibility win ABM want, LASER adopt pattern. calc_capacity_cdr (peak-living capacity bound). laser-core sizes cumulative births, mortality-aware peak-living bound strictly better allocation squash-reclaimed long runs. R0 = β·D ordering discipline. “read settled [t], place calc_foi immediately transmission” rule eliminates subtle β·(D−1) bias. laser-generic’s component ordering audited Kermack–McKendrick final size; exhibits --one, adopt ordering fix. single, documented per-tick ordering works across whole family (rather per-model sequencing) reduces class bugs worth importing convention.

title: Bringing the LASER architecture to R: an assessment of Razer
text: blunt question R user: use Razer, call LASER R via reticulate? case reticulate + LASER (today, serious work): stronger. get actual toolkit — full component ecosystem, breadth models, active development bug-fixing, NumPy arrays reticulate hand back R native vectors/matrices, parameters/objects can introspect. price real bounded: managed Python environment, marshaling across R↔︎Python boundary, two-language debugging, reticulate’s occasional friction. anyone production spatial ABM kind LASER already supports, price buys vastly capability Razer currently offers. requirement “modeling LASER ,” reticulate + LASER capable answer right now, close feature breadth. case Razer: narrower real. wins decisively : - Python runtime unacceptable — locked-environments, pure-R deployment targets, CRAN-style distribution, teams simply maintain Python env. - model menagerie (SI…SEIRS, ± vital dynamics, importation, simple interventions) — Razer easier wiring LASER components, fully native, fast. - Teaching onboarding — articles + run_model() make Razer excellent vehicle explaining LASER’s ideas R-literate epidemiologists without Python detour. - value specific improvements Razer made (reproducibility, β·D discipline). honest verdict. Razer successful proof LASER architecture ports R, spots cleaner original. substitute LASER’s breadth, pretending otherwise users disservice. R users non-trivial LASER-style work today, reticulate + LASER pragmatic choice — get real, maintained toolkit. Razer earns place (1) Python-free option model menagerie covers, (2) first-class teaching tool, (3) design laboratory whose better ideas flow back upstream. long-term relevance depends almost entirely two things: whether grows genuine component framework (without stays fixed menagerie), whether enough R users actually need native-R ABM justify maintaining parallel implementation rather investing effort making reticulate + LASER seamless. neither holds, Razer’s durable contribution may upstream backports §7 — still make experiment worthwhile.
